title: Fatty Crumbly Brazilian Pork Patty
description: A mouthwatering Brazilian dish perfect for satisfying late-night cravings, made with ground pork, onions, and spices, all in one pan.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground pork, chopped onion, minced garlic, salt, black pepper, cumin, and paprika. Mix well until just combined.
Add the egg to the bowl and mix until the egg is fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth, but still slightly crumbly.
Using your hands, add the breadcrumbs to the bowl and mix until the mixture is just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Divide the mixture into 4 equal portions and shape each portion into a ball. Flatten each ball slightly into a patty shape.
Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add a small amount of oil to the skillet.
When the oil is hot, add 2 patties to the skillet and cook for 4 minutes on the first side, or until the edges start to brown.
Flip the patties and cook for an additional 4 minutes, or until the second side is also browned.
Serve the patties hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
